title: Global competition for battery storage
snippet: Britain is building batteries faster than any other part of its power system. Installed capacity has tripled in just three years. Batteries can now discharge more power than the entire nuclear fleet, a sign of how quickly the grid is changing. Since construction of Hinkley Point C began, Britain has installed nearly 7 GW of batteries. Hinkley Point’s 3.2 GW of
ai_summary: Analysis of rapid battery storage deployment in GB affecting grid composition and flexibility markets. Relevant to tem's understanding of evolving grid dynamics and flex/trading opportunities.
ai_reasoning: Competitor opinion piece (Drax) on battery storage trends. Provides market context on grid transformation but no direct business impact or tem-specific information. Background reading on generation mix shifts.
